search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae athlete foot telehealth guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: vaginal candidiasis guidance as skin-candidiasis coverage
tinea-capitis-only guidance as broad fungal coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Vaginal and cutaneous candidiasis are not interchangeable.
A scalp-only source cannot cover a broad skin-fungal workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact primarily for adult and adolescent tinea pedis; groin, body, scalp, nail, candidal, paediatric, and other fungal infections are not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth tinea-pedis assessment; partial for in-person scraping, culture, or atypical disease review.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, only in tinea-pedis-compatible contexts.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 tinea-pedis document, due for revision 2029-02-21, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA tinea-pedis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped tinea-pedis or dermatophyte terminology, itch, annular or scaly description, foot distribution, contact context, and compatible lesion findings only in its stated subtype.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fever, spreading redness, pain, discharge, face or neck spread, extensive disease, immunocompromise, secondary infection, non-response, and worsening fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed scraping, microscopy or KOH, fungal culture, and previous-treatment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented hygiene, recurrence prevention, medicine decisions, questions, return, and follow-up without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led hygiene, reinfection prevention, topical or systemic treatment choices, and reassessment; no dose-bearing content is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad fungal or candidal identity, ringworm beyond compatible descriptions, groin, body, scalp, candidiasis, contact spread, fixed two-to-four-week timing, and non-foot treatment remain unsupported.
Tinea or candidiasis remains clinician-stated; this subtype source must not be treated as broad fungal diagnosis coverage.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
